headline: Memory chip costs pressure device retailers
detail: Rising memory chip costs are squeezing margins across the hardware supply chain. This is a direct headwind for AVGO, which is already your largest losing position and flagged for trimming — cost pressure narratives can keep a stock in the penalty box even when fundamentals are solid.

headline: AI shifts from scale to efficiency
detail: OpenAI and Anthropic are pivoting away from raw compute consumption toward leaner, more efficient AI models. This matters for your book because names like PLTR and AVGO have been priced partly on the assumption that AI demand = endless compute spend — that tailwind is getting softer.

headline: S&P 500 volatility signals mixed
detail: Bulls and bears are genuinely deadlocked right now with no clear near-term direction from volatility indicators. For a margin trader concentrated in high-beta tech and chip names, a choppy, directionless tape is dangerous — you can get stopped out on noise rather than trend.

headline: SpaceX investors grapple with volatility
detail: Early SpaceX investors are dealing with big price swings tied to Elon Musk sentiment and broader narrative risk. You hold both SPCX (new IPO, very limited data) and SPAL (2x leveraged), meaning volatility in this name hits you twice as hard on SPAL — keep position sizes tight.
